Flight (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $93,772,375Details
International Box Office $66,786,063
Worldwide Box Office $160,558,438
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $22,399,505
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $10,938,127
Total $33,337,632
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Whip Whitaker is a seasoned airline pilot, who miraculously crash lands his plane after a mid-air catastrophe, saving nearly every soul on board. After the crash, Whip is hailed as a hero, but as more is learned, more questions than answers arise as to who or what was really at fault and what really happened on that plane?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$24,900,566 (26.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.77 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:58.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$31,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,884 opening theaters/2,638 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $140,837,814

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 2nd, 2012 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: November 8th, 2012 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
November 15th, 2012 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
November 22nd, 2012 (Wide) (Lebanon)
December 6th, 2012 (Wide) (Ukraine)
December 7th, 2012 (Wide) (Turkey)
Video Release: February 5th, 2013 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for drug and alcohol abuse, language, sexuality/nudity and an intense action sequence.
(Rating bulletin 2240, 9/12/2012)
Running Time: 138 minutes
Plot point: Addiction, Disaster, Lawyers
Social setting: In a Plane
Subgenre: Courtroom / Legal Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Paramount Pictures, ImageMovers, Parkes+Macdonald Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Whip Whitaker is a seasoned airline pilot, who miraculously crash lands his plane after a mid-air catastrophe, saving nearly every soul on board. After the crash, Whip is hailed as a hero, but as more is learned, more questions than answers arise as to who or what was really at fault and what really happened on that plane?

Opening Weekend:$24,900,566 (26.6% of total gross)

Theater counts:1,884 opening theaters/2,638 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $140,837,814
